content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /agent/data/agent-workflow/.claude/skills/respond-to-review

# Respond to Review

Addresses all open review comments on the implementation PR — inline review
threads (resolved via GraphQL) and general PR comments (acknowledged with a
marker reply) — then pushes changes, converts the PR back to ready-for-review,
and records a `comments_addressed` log entry in the task YAML.

## Context

All required values are provided in the agent context under **## Your claimed task**:

| Key | Description |
|---|---|
| `Workspace root` | Absolute path to the management (workspace) repo |
| `Feature` | Feature ID (e.g. `agent-pr-response`) |
| `Task ID` | Task ID (e.g. `T4`) |
| `Impl repo` | Implementation repo ID |
| `Repo root` | Absolute path to the implementation repo |
| `Branch` | Feature branch name (e.g. `feature/agent-pr-response-T4`) |
| `PR URL` | GitHub PR URL (`https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{number}`) |

The `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable is set for all GitHub API calls.

---

## Execution Steps

### Step 1 — Parse the PR URL

Extract `owner`, `repo`, and `pull_number` from the `PR URL`:

```
https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{pull_number}
```

### Step 2 — Set PR to Draft

Signal that the agent is working on this PR:

```bash
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  "https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}" \
  -d '{"draft": true}'
```

If the PR is already a draft or the call fails, continue — this is a best-effort signal.

### Step 3 — Fetch Unresolved Inline Review Threads via GraphQL

```bash
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
  -d '{
    "query": "query($owner:String!,$name:String!,$number:Int!){repository(owner:$owner,name:$name){pullRequest(number:$number){reviewThreads(first:100){nodes{id isResolved comments(first:10){nodes{databaseId body author{login}}}}}}}}",
    "variables": {"owner": "{owner}", "name": "{repo}", "number": {pull_number}}
  }'
```

From the response, build one list:

**Unresolved inline threads** (`reviewThreads.nodes` where `isResolved: false`):
- Each thread has an `id` (node ID) needed for resolving
- The thread's first comment describes the requested change

General PR-level issue comments are not tracked — only inline review threads are addressed.

If the list is empty, skip to Step 7.

### Step 4 — Check Out the Implementation Branch

```bash
cd <Repo root>
git fetch origin
git checkout <Branch>
git reset --hard origin/<Branch>
```

### Step 5 — Address Each Comment

For each unresolved inline thread:
1. Read the thread's first comment to understand the requested change.
2. Edit the referenced file and line.
3. Stage and commit:
   ```bash
   git add <changed-files>
   git commit -m "fix: address review comment — <brief description>"
   ```

Batch closely related changes into a single commit. Unrelated changes must be
separate commits. After addressing all comments, run any available type-check or
lint commands to confirm no regressions (e.g. `npm run typecheck` or `tsc --noEmit`).

### Step 6 — Push Changes

```bash
git push origin <Branch>
```

Stop and report if the push is rejected. Do not update the task YAML.

### Step 7 — Resolve Each Inline Thread

For each thread that was addressed, resolve it via GraphQL:

```bash
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://api.github.com/graphql" \
  -d '{"query":"mutation($threadId:ID!){resolveReviewThread(input:{threadId:$threadId}){thread{isResolved}}}","variables":{"threadId":"{thread.id}"}}'
```

### Step 8 — Convert PR to Ready-for-Review

```bash
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  "https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}" \
  -d '{"draft": false}'
```

Verify the response contains `"draft": false`. If the API returns an error,
log the details and continue — the task YAML must still be updated.

### Step 9 — Update the Task YAML in the Management Repo

Read the task YAML:
```
<Workspace root>/docs/features/<Feature>/tasks/<Task ID>.yaml
```

Append to the `log` array:
```yaml
- action: comments_addressed
  by: <GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL>
  at: <ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone>
  note: "Resolved N inline review thread(s); PR converted to ready-for-review."
```

Also update:
- `execution.last_updated_by` → `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL`
- `execution.last_updated_at` → current ISO-8601 timestamp

Write the updated YAML back to the same path.

### Step 10 — Commit and Push the Task YAML

In the management repo:
```bash
cd <Workspace root>
git fetch origin
git checkout <Branch>
git reset --hard origin/<Branch>
git add docs/features/<Feature>/tasks/<Task ID>.yaml
git commit -m "chore(<Task ID>): comments_addressed — PR converted to ready-for-review"
git push origin <Branch>
```

---

## No-comment fast path

If Step 3 finds no unresolved inline threads:
- Skip Steps 4–7
- Still perform Step 8 (ensure PR is ready-for-review — it may have been left in draft)
- Write the log entry with note "No unresolved threads found; PR confirmed ready-for-review"

---

## Error Handling

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| GraphQL query fails | Stop; report the error. Do not attempt to address anything. |
| `git push` rejected on impl branch | Stop; report the error. Do not update task YAML. |
| `resolveReviewThread` mutation fails | Log the error in the `comments_addressed` note; continue with remaining threads |
| PR already not in draft state (Step 8) | Skip Step 8; still write `comments_addressed` log entry |
| Task YAML parse error | Stop and report — do not write malformed YAML |

---

## Hard Stop Rule — Missing Tools

If any shell command exits with code 127 ("command not found"), do NOT attempt
to install it or work around it. Stop immediately and report which tool is missing.

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services plus RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
# The RAG stack is included by default — all services start with `docker compose up --build`.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is enabled by default. Required:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow").
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values (including RAG vars)
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents + RAG stack (default)
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — enabled by default. Agents query the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task.
    # Defaults to the companion rag-server in this compose file.
    # Override in .env to point to an external RAG server, or set to empty to disable.
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services plus RAG stack (qdrant, rag-server, indexer).
# The RAG stack is included by default — all services start with `docker compose up --build`.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is enabled by default. Required:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow").
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values (including RAG vars)
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents + RAG stack (default)

    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow

    # RAG context injection — enabled by default. Agents query the RAG server

    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro

  deploy:
    resources:

        memory: 10G

        memory: 2G
